name: Pipeline-Oriented Programming

corePrinciples: Compose small stages into a linear flow
Each stage is a pure transformation
x.controlDataFlow: Value flows left-to-right through composed stage functions; result of one is the input of the next.
x.suitableProjects: Data transformation, request handling, ETL, and validation flows expressed as a sequence of steps.

def: A code-level style that structures computation as data flowing through a linear sequence of composable transformation stages.
problem: Nested calls and temporary variables obscure a fundamentally sequential transformation.
mechanism: Stages are composed (pipe/`|>`, method chaining, or bind) so each consumes the previous output. "Railway-oriented" pipelines thread a Result so any stage’s error short-circuits the rest.
limits: Deep pipelines can hide branching logic
failures: Swallowing errors instead of propagating them
whenNot: Performance paths where stage overhead matters
myths: "Pipelines are just method chaining" — the key is composition + uniform error handling
